native_name: มอเก็น (ชาวเล)
textile_history: The Moklen are sea nomads who traditionally had minimal textile production. They traded for cloth with mainland communities. Their material culture focused on boat-building and fishing rather than weaving.
cultural_significance: Moklen patterns, when present, reflect marine life and ocean rhythms. The wave pattern is central to their visual culture, appearing on traded garments they adopted.
